description: Deeper follow-up audit (round 2) after the 2026-07-19 pass that fixed BUG-1..6. Scope: quality-tier (P3-P4) and integration/edge-case categories the first pass did not exhaustively sweep - N+1/perf, resource/log hygiene, boundary and concurrency edge cases beyond what prior GAP waves already closed. Each story is find-and-fix within one category, not a fixed bug list: audit that category first, write a failing test per confirmed defect, fix minimally, then mark the story passing. If a category turns up nothing after genuine audit effort, mark passes true with notes explaining what was checked and why nothing qualified - do not fabricate a fix.

userStories.title: N+1 queries and missing eager-loading on listing/detail endpoints
userStories.description: As an API/web consumer, list and detail endpoints (households, locations, shelves, products, members) must not issue per-row queries for relations already known to be needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Enable query logging (DB::listen or a test assertion helper) in a temporary test and audit every index/show controller under src/Http/Controllers for N+1 patterns
Any confirmed N+1 gets a regression test asserting a bounded query count and a with()/withCount() fix
userStories.notes: Audited index/show on all Api and Web controllers plus their Resources/views. One confirmed N+1: HouseholdController::index -> HouseholdResource -> Household::roleOf() re-queried inventory_household_user per household (memoization was per-instance only, not shared with the pivot already loaded via $user->households()). Fixed by having roleOf() reuse the already-hydrated pivot relation when it belongs to the same user. Regression test: tests/Feature/HouseholdPolicyRolesTest.php::test_role_of_reuses_the_pivot_already_loaded_via_user_households (asserts flat query count across 5 households). LocationController/ShelfController/ProductController/MemberController/SearchController/AdminController index/show and all audited Web controllers already eager-load or withCount() correctly - no other N+1 found.

userStories.title: Sensitive data in logs and error responses
userStories.description: As a security-conscious operator, application logs and error payloads (client-error intake, exception handler, admin API) must never contain tokens, passwords, or full auth headers.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit config/logging.php, the exception handler, the client-error intake controller, and any Log::/logger() calls for accidental secret/PII logging
Audit request/response logging middleware if any exists for header redaction
Any confirmed leak gets a regression test and a minimal redaction fix
userStories.notes: Genuine audit, no fix needed. This package has zero Log::/logger()/->log() calls anywhere in src/ - logging is entirely the host app's responsibility (this is a library, not a standalone app, per CLAUDE.md), so config/logging.php doesn't exist here either. Checked: (1) ClientErrorController - client-supplied message/error_code/app_version are validated (max lengths) and stored only in inventory_client_errors table, never logged or echoed back; response is a bare 201 with no body. (2) InventoryServiceProvider's only exception-handler customization is a renderable() for AuthenticationException that returns a static 'Unauthenticated.' message - no exception message/trace ever surfaced. (3) AdminController (admin API) - user/household payloads are built via explicit allow-listed arrays (userPayload/householdPayload), never a raw model dump; User model has password and remember_token in $hidden, and AdminController's payload builder doesn't touch them. (4) AuthController/WebAuthController/WebGoogleAuthController - only static messages returned ('Invalid Google token.', 'Logged out.'), no token/credential echoed. (5) EnsureAdminToken middleware compares tokens via hash_equals and returns static 401/503 messages, never reflects the provided token. (6) No request/response logging middleware exists in src/Http/Middleware/. Full PHPUnit suite passes (381 tests, 1294 assertions).

userStories.title: Numeric/boundary edge cases in stock and pagination
userStories.description: As a user, stock add/remove amounts and any paginated/limited endpoints must handle boundary values (0, negative, very large, non-integer, missing) without a 500 or silent wrong behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit AmountRequest-style form requests and any limit/page query params for missing bounds validation
Any confirmed gap gets a regression test (expect 422, not 500) and a minimal validation fix
userStories.notes: Audited ProductRequest (quantity/low_stock_threshold - already bounded via MAX_QUANTITY, W14), the amount() helper in ProductController (already bounded), ReorderRequest (already capped at MAX_IDS/500 ids), and every other FormRequest in src/Http/Requests for missing bounds. Confirmed gap: ShelfRequest's `position` field had `integer|min:0` with no upper bound, while the underlying column is `unsignedInteger` (~4.29B ceiling) - the same class of bug W14 already fixed for product quantity, just missed here. Regression test tests/Feature/ResourceCrudTest.php::test_shelf_position_beyond_the_column_ceiling_is_rejected (POST a shelf with position=5_000_000_000, expect 422) failed before the fix (SQLite silently stored it and returned 201). Fixed by adding max:ProductRequest::MAX_QUANTITY to ShelfRequest::rules(), reusing the existing bound constant. Also checked LocationRequest: its `position` column exists but is never client-writable (only set via ReorderRequest, which is already bounded), so no gap there. Pagination/limit params (SearchController, WebSearchController, AdminController) are all hardcoded ->limit(50), not user-controlled, so no boundary issue. Full PHPUnit suite passes (382 tests, 1297 assertions); Pint and Larastan (max level) clean.

userStories.title: Dead code, deprecated Laravel/PHP APIs, resource leaks
userStories.description: As a maintainer, the codebase should not carry unreachable code paths, deprecated framework calls, or unclosed resources (file handles, DB transactions left open on early return).
acceptanceCriteria: Grep for deprecated Laravel 13 APIs and PHP 8.3 deprecation notices (run with error_reporting tuned to surface deprecations if feasible)
Check every early-return path inside DB::transaction closures and file-handling code (image upload, export) for leaks
Any confirmed issue gets a minimal fix; unreachable code is deleted with justification in notes, not just flagged
Full PHPUnit suite, Larastan, and Pint stay clean
